title: Date picker vs date input vs select vs text input
summary: Use a date picker when users choose a single date with calendar context, availability, or day-of-week meaning; use date input for known exact dates, select for short fixed date choices, and text input for fuzzy date wording.
decisionRules: Prefer date picker when the user is scheduling an appointment, choosing from availability, or comparing a date against nearby weekdays.
Prefer date input when the date is already known, memorable, printed on a document, or far enough in the past that month-by-month navigation would be slow.
Prefer select when the valid answer is a short controlled list such as available appointment slots, reporting periods, or school years.
Prefer text input when the product accepts an approximate answer such as early July, Spring 2026, next quarter, or unknown day.
Keep a visible text field with a date-format hint so keyboard users can type the date instead of using the calendar menu.
Expose unavailable, before-minimum, after-maximum, today, focused, selected, open, closed, and error states because calendar choice depends on visible constraints.
Use previous and next month controls when nearby dates matter; do not make users page through decades for a birthday or other remembered historic date.
Return focus to the input or selected calendar day after a selection, month change, validation error, or close action.
Do not submit automatically when a day is highlighted or selected; selection is not the same as final form submission.
Explain disabled dates in plain language, such as weekends unavailable or outside the booking window, rather than leaving blank inactive cells.
failureModes: A birthday flow opens on the current month and forces dozens of year changes instead of accepting typed date parts.
Unavailable days look like ordinary choices and only fail after submission.
The calendar menu opens without a visible label or format hint, so manual typing and screen reader context are weak.
Month navigation changes the grid without announcing the new month or preserving the selected date.
The component traps focus inside the calendar or drops focus at the top of the page after selection.
A highlighted day auto-submits before the user can review the rest of the form.

sources.note: USWDS supports date picker use for scheduling, day-of-week context, manual typing, min and max bounds, keyboard navigation, and avoiding auto-submission.
Carbon supports date picker anatomy, calendar menu open state, month and year controls, calendar day states, validation, and localization.
CMS supports calendar picker use for recent or future dates, flexible typed entry, memorable-date alternatives, focus return, and disabled-date announcements.
